llm_reasoning: The location is highly unsuitable for groundnuts. The mean annual temperature (8.241°C) is critically low, indicating an extremely short, cool growing season insufficient for groundnut maturity (optimal 25-30°C). Annual rainfall (339.815 mm) is severely deficient, requiring extensive irrigation. The clay content (26%) is high, hindering pegging and harvesting. Strongest positive: Ideal soil pH (6.67). Strongest limiting: Insufficient heat units and severe water deficit. Operational risks include crop failure due to lack of maturity and water stress. Mitigation is not feasible for field-scale production.
